1.template: according to the commandment of my father, {0}

1.title: Why Jarom keeps it short
1.text: Jarom explains he won't record his own prophecies or revelations—his fathers had already revealed the plan of salvation, and ‘this sufficeth me.’ His whole book is just 15 verses.

1.question: At about two hundred years, how does Jarom describe the people of Nephi?
options: Strong in the land, keeping the law of Moses and the sabbath

1.question: How were the Nephites able to withstand the Lamanites in battle?
options: Their kings and leaders were mighty in the faith of the Lord

1.title: Iron, copper, brass, and steel
1.text: Jarom lists the Nephites' skill in metalwork—gold, silver, iron, copper, brass, and steel—shaping both tools to till the ground and weapons of war like the arrow, dart, and javelin.

1.template: Inasmuch as ye will keep my commandments ye shall {0} in the land

1.question: What did the prophets, priests, and teachers persuade the people to do?
options: Look forward to the Messiah and believe in him to come
Make peace treaties with the Lamanites

1.title: The plates grow full
1.text: Jarom points readers to the ‘other plates of Nephi’ for the record of their wars, then hands these small plates to his son Omni—the record passing down a family line that keeps shrinking its words.

1.template: I deliver these plates into the hands of my son {0}
